title: Accessibility Check
summary: Review course materials for accessibility barriers and surface practical revision suggestions against WCAG-style checks.
inputCaption: Educators can upload files directly or pull materials from Canvas before running a lightweight accessibility audit demo.

materialOptions.label: Upload course materials
materialOptions.description: Bring in lecture slides, handouts, PDFs, or image-based materials.

materialOptions.label: Get materials from Canvas
materialOptions.description: Pull a prepared package of pages, files, and slides from the LMS.

outputCaption: The demo returns a sample compliance score first, followed by example issues that an educator could revise.
emptyOutputText: Choose a source for course materials, then run the audit to reveal the sample accessibility review.
complianceSummary: Most materials meet baseline accessibility expectations, with a few revisions recommended before sharing broadly.

issues.title: Low contrast may be difficult for color-blind learners
issues.description: A light green heading sits on a pale gray background, reducing readability for learners with low vision or color-contrast needs.
issues.recommendation: Increase the text-to-background contrast and avoid relying on hue alone to signal importance.

issues.title: Image is missing alt text
issues.description: The infographic communicates key deadlines visually, but screen-reader users would not receive the same information.
issues.recommendation: Add concise alt text that explains the main dates and instructional purpose of the image.

highlights.label: Best for
highlights.value: Material prep, accessibility review, and inclusive course design.

highlights.label: Output shape
highlights.value: Flagged issues, accessibility notes, and revision recommendations.
